title_original: Conductor Quantum Introduces Coda, a Natural Language Interface for Quantum Computing - InfoQ
summary_original: Conductor Quantum has launched Coda, a natural language interface allowing users to describe quantum computing tasks in everyday language, which the system translates into quantum circuits and executes on real hardware. At launch, Coda supports Rigetti’s 84-qubit processor and simulates up to 34 qubits using NVIDIA's cuQuantum and CUDA-Q. The platform aims to lower the barrier to entry by eliminating the need for specialized programming knowledge, acting as an orchestration layer above existing software development kits and hardware. Coda offers a learn mode for transparency in circuit construction and interpretation. This software release addresses the lag in quantum software tooling compared to recent hardware advances, drawing on Conductor Quantum’s prior work in chip control and hardware collaboration.
